Now for some of you it may feel like fluff but I'm here to tell you you know with all my successes I've had with Facebook ads, with TV ads, with email marketing campaigns, affiliate marketing, SEO everything has been done from a marketing perspective and really understanding how do I get people from different steps of the journey. So if you've been through some of my funnel guides you'll know that my goal from the very first ad isn't to get something to buy because it's impossible they're not going to see the ad and throw their credit card at you but I do want to open them up. So you know when I think about audiences there's two things I'm looking at. How aware are they of my brand or my product? So let's say I've started a brand new e-commerce store that's fairly low awareness then the other side I'm looking at is intent. So if I go after a completely cold audience they've never heard of my website or seen my website before they have low intent and low awareness so the prospect is not aware of my solution and they're not in market for my solution either because I can't go out there and say here's this amazing watch and you should buy it. Their intent at that moment in time is probably fairly low. Now obviously with audiences you can overlay intent you can put people that have recently used a credit card, people that are engaged shoppers and you know I'm hoping by the time you read this Facebook haven't actually removed those as attributes and behaviors but you can build intents okay you go a bit further along. step now going into the actual content there's three Rs that I focus on relationship rapport so as you can see here relationship is I'm here for you and I'm actually starting that communication working that through and seeing if I can actually generate what you know wherever my funnel is start to build those goals there and relevance is delivering those ads at the right time so the right out ## [18:20 - 21:21]

because that for me is a way of maximizing the data and the performance Facebook loves great data going into your website coming out of your account so if I'm getting a so this is this isn't even an example if I'm getting a low CTR let's say 0.8 percent or 1 percent but the people that click are having a great experience on my site my bounce rates are low my conversion rates are high that counts for example I've heard people talk about running traffic campaigns they just want a pile of traffic from their audiences into the site and then what they do is retarget them the problem with that is ## [21:21 - 22:16]

works far better when their intent is higher and their awareness is better as well because if you start running FOMO here they don't know who you are they don't really know about your product they don't trust you you've not really sold them on anything and you say hey only five left hurry up and buy you know what they're just going to skip it because that doesn't mean anything to them so high intent or at least higher intent and higher awareness makes a big difference with that as well then looking at this this is really the core of where I spend a lot of my time I'm going to break this out into a separate video because the introductory part is already quite long and I know how hard it is sometimes to break these things out so this video has been about introducing you to how I see my content campaign planning in my next video I'm going to talk about the 5w avatar and how you can actually apply that for your business
